syllabus.topic: Linear Regression Fundamentals and Problem Setup
sub_points: Regression problem definition and how it differs from classification (output type, loss, evaluation)
Linear regression model form, notation/terminology (features, weights, bias, design matrix), and general multivariate cases
Relationship between linear regression and logistic regression; when each is appropriate
Logistic regression as a probabilistic model for classification using a link function (sigmoid) and decision boundary interpretation

syllabus.topic: Fitting Linear Models: Likelihood, Least Squares, and Geometry
sub_points: Parameter estimation workflow for linear regression models (objective functions and optimization perspective)
Negative log-likelihood (NLL) viewpoint and its connection to common regression assumptions (e.g., Gaussian noise)
Least-squares estimation (LSE) recap for linear classifiers and extension to linear regression
Geometric interpretation of LSE (projection onto column space, residual orthogonality) and implications for solutions

syllabus.topic: Extensions and Diagnostics: Weighted Least Squares and Goodness of Fit
sub_points: Weighted least squares to handle heteroscedasticity or varying sample reliability
Goodness-of-fit metrics (error summaries and variance-explained style measures) and their interpretation
Residual analysis via residual plots to diagnose model mismatch, nonlinearity, and outliers
Practical considerations in assessing model adequacy beyond a single scalar metric

syllabus.topic: Regularization for Linear Regression: Ridge and Lasso
sub_points: Limitations of ordinary LSE (overfitting, multicollinearity, ill-conditioning) motivating regularization
Ridge regression (L2 regularization): objective, shrinkage effect, and selecting/deriving an optimal weight
Under-determined systems and the small-sample/high-dimensional regime; ridge as a stabilizer and its optimality conditions
More extreme regularization leading to sparsity: lasso regression (L1) and its Bayesian interpretation via a Laplace prior
Comparative behavior of ridge vs. lasso (shrinkage vs. feature selection) and practical trade-offs

syllabus.topic: Tree-Based Methods: Regression/Classification Trees and CART Model Fitting
sub_points: Motivation for tree models as nonparametric learners and how they partition feature space
Regression trees with worked examples and visualization of piecewise-constant/piecewise-simple predictions
Classification trees and the unified CART perspective across regression and classification
CART training procedure: node splitting criteria, greedy search, and selecting the optimal tree/model
Special-case handling and a balanced view of decision trees’ strengths and weaknesses (interpretability vs. variance/instability)

syllabus.topic: Ensemble Learning for Stabilizing Trees: Bagging and Random Forests
sub_points: Instability of single decision trees (e.g., sensitivity to data perturbations) and empirical demonstration (IRIS example)
Core ensemble ideas: combining multiple models and voting/aggregation mechanisms
Bagging (bootstrap aggregating): algorithm, bias-variance intuition, and pros/cons in practice
Random forests: decorrelating base trees via feature subsampling; end-to-end training process and example
Performance comparison/visualization of random forests vs. bagging and when RF typically provides gains

source_file: Lecture 4 Linear regression and Nonparametric methods.pdf
